title: DeathBroadcast
description: Broadcasts your death coordinates in chat (X, Y, Z + world)
body:
DeathBroadcast is a lightweight, no-config, quality-of-life plugin for Minecraft servers.
Whenever a player dies, their exact death location (X, Y, Z) and the world they were in (Overworld, Nether, End) will be broadcasted in chat to all players.

# **⁉️ Why ?**
This removes the guesswork of where someone perished and makes recovering lost items much easier.

The player you see in the screenshot below, is the one that kept dying without knowing where. He's my source of inspiration for creating this plugin.

![screenshot of the chat message of a player dying in the nether](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6537bb183190392a2d81c18809651959fc79abfd.png)

# **✅ Features**
- Shows exact death coordinates (X, Y, Z) in chat.
- Displays the world name (Overworld, Nether, End).
- Lightweight — no configuration needed.
- Small delay ensures the broadcast appears after the vanilla death message.
- Fully server-side — no mods or client installs required.

# **🛠️ Installation**
- Download the latest .jar file.
- Drop it into your server’s plugins folder.
- Restart your server — done!

# **⚡ Compatibility**
Works on Paper 1.20.x and above (so 1.21.x) as well as on forks like Purpur/Folia).
